session.user:
Update help strings in non-exec CLI command files. Change "plan" references to "PR" where "plan" refers to an erk-plan (NOT when it refers to Claude plan mode, erk-plan label, plan.md filename, plan-header, workflow names, branch prefixes, variable/function/class names, lifecycle stage "planning", or the verb "plan"/"planning").
Make these specific edits:
1. `src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py:102` — change `help="Create a plan remotely without implementing it"` → `help="Create a PR remotely without implementing it"`
2. `src/erk/cli/commands/reconcile_cmd.py:26` — change `help="Skip creating learn plans"` → `help="Skip creating learn PRs"`
3. `src/erk/cli/commands/land_cmd.py:1531` — change `help="Skip creating a learn plan after landing."` → `help="Skip creating a learn PR after landing."`
4. `src/erk/cli/commands/admin.py:419` — change `help="Existing plan number to use"` → `help="Existing PR number to use"`
5. `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/create_cmd.py`: - Line 25: `help="Plan file to read"` → `help="PR file to read"` - Line 27: `help="Plan title (default: extract from H1)"` → `help="PR title (default: extract from H1)"` - Line 29: `help="AI-generated summary to display above the collapsed plan"` → `help="AI-generated summary to display above the collapsed PR"`
6. `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/duplicate_check_cmd.py`: - Line 26: `help="Plan file to read"` → `help="PR file to read"` - Line 32: `help="Existing plan ID to check (fetches body and excludes self from comparison)"` → `help="Existing PR ID to check (fetches body and excludes self from comparison)"`
7. `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py`: - Line 122: `help="Show plans from all users (default: show only your plans)"` → `help="Show PRs from all users (default: show only your PRs)"` - Line 128: `help="Sort order: by plan number (default) or recent branch activity"` → `help="Sort order: by PR number (default) or recent branch activity"`
8. `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/view_cmd.py`: - Line 280: `" Returns plan title, state, labels, timestamps, and header metadata."` → `" Returns PR title, state, labels, timestamps, and header metadata."` - Line 286: `help="Show full plan body"` → `help="Show full PR body"`
9. `src/erk/cli/commands/branch/create_cmd.py:40` — change `help="Plan number or URL with erk-plan label"` → `help="PR number or URL with erk-plan label"`
10. `src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py:453` — change `help="Plan number or PR with erk-plan label"` → `help="PR number or URL with erk-plan label"`
11. `src/erk/cli/commands/branch/delete_cmd.py:348` — change `help="Also close associated PR and plan."` → `help="Also close associated PRs."`
12. `src/erk/cli/commands/wt/create_cmd.py:454` — change `help="Copy the plan file instead of moving it (requires --from-plan-file)."` → `help="Copy the PR file instead of moving it (requires --from-plan-file)."`
13. `src/erk/cli/commands/wt/delete_cmd.py:534` — change `help="Delete branch, close associated PR and plan."` → `help="Delete branch, close associated PRs."`
Read each file first, then use the Edit tool to make exact replacements. Be careful to match the exact existing strings.
session.assistant: I'll start by reading all the files that need to be edited to verify the exact strings that need to be changed.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Command to dispatch a task for fully autonomous remote execution. 2→ 3→No local planning — the remote Claude explores the codebase, plans the change, 4→implements it, and creates a PR. 5→ 6→Usage: 7→ erk one-shot "fix the import in config.py" 8→ erk one-shot --file prompt.md 9→ erk one-shot "add type hints to utils.py" --model opus 10→ erk one-shot "fix the typo in README.md" --dry-run 11→ erk one-shot "rename issue_number to plan_number in impl_init.py" --plan-only 12→ erk one-shot "fix config bug" --repo owner/repo 13→""" 14→ 15→from pathlib import Path 16→ 17→import click 18→ 19→from erk.cli.commands.implement_shared import normalize_model_name 20→from erk.cli.commands.one_shot_remote_dispatch import ( 21→ OneShotDispatchParams, 22→ OneShotDispatchResult, 23→ OneShotDryRunResult, 24→ dispatch_one_shot_remote, 25→) 26→from erk.cli.commands.ref_resolution import resolve_dispatch_ref 27→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure, UserFacingCliError 28→from erk.core.context import ErkContext, NoRepoSentinel 29→from erk_shared.agentclick.json_command import json_command 30→from erk_shared.agentclick.mcp_exposed import mcp_exposed
... [208 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Reconcile locally-tracked branches that were merged remotely. 2→ 3→Detects branches whose PRs were merged outside `erk land` (e.g., via GitHub 4→web UI, mobile, or API) and runs the same post-merge lifecycle: learn PR 5→creation, objective updates, and branch/worktree cleanup. 6→""" 7→ 8→import click 9→from rich.console import Console 10→from rich.table import Table 11→ 12→from erk.cli.commands.reconcile_pipeline import ( 13→ ReconcileBranchInfo, 14→ ReconcileResult, 15→ detect_merged_branches, 16→ process_merged_branch, 17→) 18→from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context 19→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 20→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 21→ 22→ 23→@click.command("reconcile", hidden=True) 24→@click.option("--force", is_flag=True, help="Skip confirmation prompts") 25→@click.option("--dry-run", is_flag=True, help="Preview without making changes") 26→@click.option("--skip-learn", is_flag=True, help="Skip creating learn plans") 27→@click.pass_obj 28→def reconcile(ctx: ErkContext, *, force: bool, dry_run: bool, skip_learn: bool) -> None: 29→ """Reconcile branches merged outside erk land. 30→

157→ except RuntimeError: 163→ """Prune worktree metadata, ignoring errors if nothing to prune. 168→ and failure doesn't affect the primary operation, we allow silent failure. 172→ except Exception: 225→ f"Warning: Worktree {name} is in detached HEAD state. Cannot delete branch.", 320→ This function encapsulates the legitimate error boundary for shutil.rmtree 363→ except OSError: 372→def _delete_branch_at_error_boundary( 377→ This function encapsulates a legitimate error boundary because: 381→ 4. This is a CLI error boundary - appropriate place per AGENTS.md 383→ The exception handling distinguishes between user-declined (expected) and 384→ actual errors (propagated as SystemExit). 386→ Note: run_subprocess_with_context catches CalledProcessError and re-raises 387→ as RuntimeError with the original exception in __cause__. 396→ except RuntimeError as e: 397→ _handle_branch_deletion_error(e, branch, force) 400→def _handle_branch_deletion_error(e: RuntimeError, branch: str, force: bool) -> None: 401→ """Handle errors from branch deletion commands. 403→ This function encapsulates the error boundary logic for branch deletion. 405→ prompt, which is expected behavior. Other errors are propagated as SystemExit. 408→ e: RuntimeError from run_subprocess_with_context, with the original 409→ CalledProcessError accessible via e.__cause__ 410→ branch: Name of the branch that failed to delete 415→ # Extract returncode from the original CalledProcessError in __cause__ 417→ if isinstance(e.__cause__, subprocess.CalledProcessError): 421→ # User declined - this is expected behavior, not an error 424→ # Other error (branch doesn't exist, git failure, etc.) 425→ # The RuntimeError message already contains stderr from run_subprocess_with_context 427→ click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"Failed to delete branch {branch_text}: {e}" 506→ _delete_branch_at_error_boundary(

1→"""Unified land command for PRs. 2→ 3→This command merges a PR and cleans up the worktree/branch. 4→It accepts a branch name, PR number, or PR URL as argument. 5→ 6→Usage: 7→ erk land # Land current branch's PR 8→ erk land --stack # Land the current Graphite stack 9→ erk land 123 # Land PR by number 10→ erk land <url> # Land PR by URL 11→ erk land <branch> # Land PR for branch 12→""" 13→ 14→import re 15→from dataclasses import dataclass, replace 16→from enum import Enum, auto 17→from pathlib import Path 18→from typing import Literal, NamedTuple, assert_never 19→ 20→import click 21→ 22→from erk.cli.activation import print_temp_script_instructions, render_activation_script 23→from erk.cli.commands.checkout_helpers import ensure_branch_has_worktree 24→from erk.cli.commands.land_pipeline import ( 25→ LandError, 26→ make_execution_state, 27→ make_initial_state, 28→ run_execution_pipeline, 29→ run_validation_pipeline, 30→)
... [1661 lines omitted] ...
239→ click.style("Warning:", fg="yellow") 609→ # Handle rate limit errors gracefully - this is an advisory check. 614→ except RuntimeError as e: 615→ error_str = str(e) 616→ if "RATE_LIMIT" in error_str or "rate limit" in error_str.lower(): 622→ raise # Re-raise other errors 667→ SystemExit(1) on validation failure 721→ SystemExit(1) on validation failure 766→ validation_error = validate_parent_is_trunk( 772→ validation_error is None, 773→ validation_error.message if validation_error else "", 814→ SystemExit(1) on validation failure 869→ SystemExit(1) on validation failure 962→ if isinstance(result, LandError): 963→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + result.message) 1047→ SystemExit(1) on validation failure 1237→ # Use --downstack to only restack the current branch, avoiding errors if 1271→ click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") 1281→ except RuntimeError: 1284→ click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") 1285→ + "git pull failed (try running manually)" 1367→ # Silence unused parameter warnings - these are still needed for the function 1398→PR_NUMBER="${{1:?Error: PR number required}}" 1399→BRANCH="${{2:?Error: Branch name required}}" 1476→ if isinstance(result, LandError): 1477→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + result.message) 1645→ if isinstance(result, LandError): 1647→ # Type narrowing: Ensure.invariant exits on failure, so result is LandState here 1648→ assert not isinstance(result, LandError)

session.assistant:
Perfect! All edits have been completed successfully. Let me summarize what was changed:
## Summary
Successfully updated help strings in 13 non-exec CLI command files, changing references from "plan" to "PR" where appropriate (only when "plan" refers to an erk-plan, not when it refers to Claude plan mode, labels, filenames, or other contexts):
### Files Modified:
1. **`src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py:102`** - Changed "Create a plan remotely" → "Create a PR remotely"
2. **`src/erk/cli/commands/reconcile_cmd.py:26`** - Changed "Skip creating learn plans" → "Skip creating learn PRs"
3. **`src/erk/cli/commands/land_cmd.py:1531`** - Changed "Skip creating a learn plan after landing" → "Skip creating a learn PR after landing"
4. **`src/erk/cli/commands/admin.py:419`** - Changed "Existing plan number to use" → "Existing PR number to use"
5. **`src/erk/cli/commands/pr/create_cmd.py`** (3 changes): - Line 25: "Plan file to read" → "PR file to read" - Line 27: "Plan title" → "PR title" - Line 29: "above the collapsed plan" → "above the collapsed PR"
6. **`src/erk/cli/commands/pr/duplicate_check_cmd.py`** (2 changes): - Line 26: "Plan file to read" → "PR file to read" - Line 32: "Existing plan ID" → "Existing PR ID"
7. **`src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py`** (2 changes): - Line 122: "Show plans from all users (default: show only your plans)" → "Show PRs from all users (default: show only your PRs)" - Line 128: "Sort order: by plan number" → "Sort order: by PR number"
8. **`src/erk/cli/commands/pr/view_cmd.py`** (2 changes): - Line 280: "Returns plan title" → "Returns PR title" - Line 286: "Show full plan body" → "Show full PR body"
9. **`src/erk/cli/commands/branch/create_cmd.py:40`** - Changed "Plan number or URL" → "PR number or URL"
10. **`src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py:453`** - Changed "Plan number or PR" → "PR number or URL"
11. **`src/erk/cli/commands/branch/delete_cmd.py:348`** - Changed "Also close associated PR and plan" → "Also close associated PRs"
12. **`src/erk/cli/commands/wt/create_cmd.py:454`** - Changed "Copy the plan file" → "Copy the PR file"
13. **`src/erk/cli/commands/wt/delete_cmd.py:534`** - Changed "Delete branch, close associated PR and plan" → "Delete branch, close associated PRs"
All edits preserved the exact context and indentation from the original files, changing only the specific help string text as requested.
